AN ELECTRONICS STORE -- CHICAGO -- MUSIC BLASTING -The Cranberries "Let It Linger" lets us know this is 1996. (Henceforth, all the music cuts will be of this vintage.) JAMIE REIDY, charmer, talker, salesman -- is doing what he does best -- selling to WOMEN. A pretty SALESGIRL catches his eye and he winks at her. IN THE TINY STOCKROOM That same salesgirl is moaning as Jamie does what he also does best...
--I can't -- wait -- oh, Jesus -squeeze the nipple -- harder -Come here--

They are awkwardly trying to fuck against a wall of cardboard cartons. He lifts her bodily onto a large vibrating speaker-Feel this--

She grabs his face and he pushes her back, her purse spilling its contents onto the floor. Her cellphone lights up. OUT IN THE SHOWROOM The store's manager, JERRY, is talking to a customer as his cellphone starts ringing.
--80 watts is more than you'll ever need for a living room -- excuse me. (answers the phone) Hello...?
He HEARS the sound of their lovemaking, looks around -IN THE SHOWROOM -- MOMENTS LATER A CROWD has formed outside the door to the stockroom. Employees and customers look around anxiously.

FIANCE!!!! SHUT UP, CHRISTY!!!! Jamie appears in the doorway, his lip bloody, his shirt torn. Pulling himself together, he walks through the crowd, his eye coming upon a college GIRL holding a stereo in its carton.

He dabs at blood dripping from his nose.
I can get it for you.
His mischievous smile shines through the blood.
Are you like okay?
What's your number -- I'll let you know when I have it.
She tilts her head -- more than a little interested. Jerry emerges from the stockroom, holding his bleeding hand.
--AND DON'T YOU EVER FUCKING COME IN HERE AGAIN!!!
(turns on him, points) You owe me commission on $54,000
worth of second-rate knock-off greymarket off-brand overpriced equipment.
I'LL FUCKING KILL YOU--Jamie gathers up his belongings, turns back to Jerry.
You can send me the check -- Christy knows the address.
Jerry lunges for him -- other employees hold him back.
(to the college girl) Nice meeting you.
As he heads to the door:
(calling after him) ....434-6603.

DR. JAMES REIDY, 65, distinguished-looking, is making drinks. He hands one to his daughter DR. HELEN REIDY, 35 and distinguished-looking, who is in mid-argument with him.

--equipment that didn't even exist when you went to medical school-JAMES, SR. --excuse me, I know I've been under a rock these past thirty years-HELEN How many trans-dermal intubations have you personally done?

Don't help him justify his laziness.

(to James, Sr.) I'm taking back her shares.

I'm killing both of you.
Their mother NANCY appears from the kitchen, her arm around FARRAH, Josh's improbably pretty wife.
We're killing all of you if you don't get in here and sit down.
And we get to keep all the shares.

Jamie's selling high-end sound equipment. Hah.

and he quits.

Nancy sees that her husband is very upset.
All right you barbarians, if you won't be still and finish this lamb none of you will get the chocolate cake.

An awkward silence descends on the table. JAMES, SR. REIDY'S STUDY -- LATER James, Sr. sits at his desk, reviewing a stack of letters. As Jamie is forced to wait, he paces, randomly fiddling with things. One crucial thing about Jamie -- he is never still.
I'm uh, I'm expecting a call actually.
This is a difficult conversation.
I don't know what conversation we're having.
You know I've tried to help you-JAMIE I haven't asked for you to-JAMES, SR. --and I'm not sure helping you was the wise thing to have done.
Here we go...
You've tried a lot of things, and I understand that not everything that happened was of your own making. The lawsuit at Shearson-JAMIE That had nothing to do with--
That's what I'm saying. But there have been many things, many things... You try things, and for whatever reason they don't turn out. This is torture for Jamie.
He closes his eyes.
Maybe if I had been more available when you were younger-Dad...
You need to understand something, Jamie. I'm not going to be able to take care of you.
I haven't asked you to-JAMES, SR. You came to me for money last year. One time.
And in '92 after the accident.
That was after an accident!
There's not going to be any more money, Jamie-I get it.
You don't get it. Look at me... You need to take responsibility for your life. There's no more your mother and I can give you.
I said I get it.
I've taken you out of our will, Jamie. I can't have you waiting for us to (MORE)
die, thinking you'll be taken care of then. The cord is cut. I should have done it a long time ago.
(stunned) ...Thank you very much.
Maybe if things change we can revisit this, but for now it's the only way. I'm sorry. Very sorry.
Jamie gets up.
And I'm sorry to have been such a disappointment to you. Jamie!
He'd like to argue, but since Jamie is right there's nothing he can say. Jamie walks out.

JAMIE
